```{r OSP-PAP_depth_profiles, DPI = 300, fig.width=8, fig.height=8, cache=FALSE}
                       values = hue_pal()(length(sizes)))+
    scale_fill_manual(breaks = sizes,
                      values = hue_pal()(length(sizes)))+
    theme_light()+
    theme(strip.text.x = element_text(size = 8, face = "bold"),
          axis.title.x = element_blank(),
          axis.title.y = element_blank(),
          plot.title = element_text(size=14, hjust=0.5))
}

trophic_depth_plots <- function(
    posts
){
  trophic.mode <- 
    subset(
      posts$trophic$mode[c(variables,"FWL","PTS","MTS")],
      Size == "0.2-0.5 mm" | Size == "1-2 mm" | Size == "2-5 mm" | Size == ">5 mm")# & Location == i)
  trophic.mode.long <-
    melt(trophic.mode,
         id.vars = c(variables),
         variable.name = "Source",
         value.name = "value")
  trophic.mode.long$Size <- factor(trophic.mode.long$Size, levels = sizes)
  trophic.HDI95 <- 
    subset(
      posts$trophic$HDI95[c(variables,"FWL","PTS","MTS")],
      Size == "0.2-0.5 mm" | Size == "1-2 mm" | Size == "2-5 mm" | Size == ">5 mm")# & Location == i)
  trophic.HDI95.long <-
    melt(trophic.HDI95,
         id.vars = c(variables),
         variable.name = "Source",
         value.name = "value")
  trophic.HDI95.long$Size <- factor(trophic.HDI95.long$Size, levels = sizes)
  trophic.HDI90 <- 
    subset(
      posts$trophic$HDI90[c(variables,"FWL","PTS","MTS")],
      Size == "0.2-0.5 mm" | Size == "1-2 mm" | Size == "2-5 mm" | Size == ">5 mm")# & Location == i)
  trophic.HDI90.long <-
    melt(trophic.HDI90,
         id.vars = c(variables),
         variable.name = "Source",
         value.name = "value")
  trophic.HDI90.long$Size <- factor(trophic.HDI90.long$Size, levels = sizes)
  trophic.HDI75 <- 
    subset(
      posts$trophic$HDI75[c(variables,"FWL","PTS","MTS")],
      Size == "0.2-0.5 mm" | Size == "1-2 mm" | Size == "2-5 mm" | Size == ">5 mm")# & Location == i)
  trophic.HDI75.long <-
    melt(trophic.HDI75,
         id.vars = c(variables),
         variable.name = "Source",
         value.name = "value")
  trophic.HDI75.long$Size <- factor(trophic.HDI75.long$Size, levels = sizes)
  trophic.HDI50 <- 
    subset(
      posts$trophic$HDI50[c(variables,"FWL","PTS","MTS")],
      Size == "0.2-0.5 mm" | Size == "1-2 mm" | Size == "2-5 mm" | Size == ">5 mm")# & Location == i)
  trophic.HDI50.long <-
    melt(trophic.HDI50,
         id.vars = c(variables),
         variable.name = "Source",
         value.name = "value")
  trophic.HDI50.long$Size <- factor(trophic.HDI50.long$Size, levels = sizes)
  
  # Appending columns with the min and max of the 95% HDI to the mode data frame.
  trophic.mode.long$min95 <- trophic.HDI95.long$trophic[seq(1,nrow(trophic.HDI95.long),2)]
  trophic.mode.long$max95 <- trophic.HDI95.long$trophic[seq(2,nrow(trophic.HDI95.long),2)]
  
  sizes <- c("0.2-0.5 mm", "1-2 mm", "2-5 mm", ">5 mm")
  
  # print(
  ggplot()+
    # geom_path(
    #   data=trophic.mode.long,
    #   aes(x=f, y=Depth, color = Size, group = interaction(Size)),
    #   size = 1, alpha = 1,
    #   position = position_dodgev(height = 100))+
    geom_line(
      data = trophic.HDI95.long,
      aes(x = value, y = Depth, color = Size, group = interaction(Depth, Size)),
      alpha = 0.5, size = 1,
      position = position_dodgev(height = 100))+
    geom_line(
      data = trophic.HDI90.long,
      aes(x = value, y = Depth, color = Size, group = interaction(Depth, Size)),
      alpha = 0.7, size = 1.5,
      position = position_dodgev(height = 100))+
    geom_line(
      data = trophic.HDI75.long,
      aes(x = value, y = Depth, color = Size, group = interaction(Depth, Size)),
      alpha = 1, size = 2,
      position = position_dodgev(height = 100))+
    geom_line(
      data = trophic.HDI50.long,
      aes(x = value, y = Depth, color = Size, group = interaction(Depth, Size)),
      alpha = 1, size = 3,
      position = position_dodgev(height = 100))+
    geom_point(
      data=trophic.mode.long,
      aes(x = value, y=Depth, fill = Size, group = interaction(Depth, Size)),
      size = 2.5, shape = 22,
      position = position_dodgev(height = 100))+
    labs()+
    facet_wrap(~ Source)+
    # facet_wrap(~ Source, nrow = 1)+
    scale_y_reverse(breaks = c(0,250,500,750,1000,1250,1500), minor_breaks = c())+
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(-1,5,1), 
                       minor_breaks = c(-0.5,5.5,1),
                       labels = c("-1","0","1","2","3","4","5"))+
    coord_cartesian(ylim=c(NA,0), xlim=c(0,NA))+
    scale_color_manual(breaks = sizes,
                       values = hue_pal()(length(sizes)))+
    scale_fill_manual(breaks = sizes,
                      values = hue_pal()(length(sizes)))+
    theme_light()+
    theme(strip.text.x = element_text(size = 8, face = "bold"),
          axis.title.x = element_blank(),
          axis.title.y = element_blank(),
          plot.title = element_text(size=14, hjust=0.5))
}

plot.PAP.f <-
  f_depth_plots(posts.PAP) + 
  ggtitle("Proportional Contributions") + 
  theme(axis.title.y = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 3)) +
  ylab("Depth (m)")
plot.OSP.f <-
  f_depth_plots(posts.OSP) + 
  ggtitle("Proportional Contributions") + 
  theme(axis.title.y = element_text(angle = 90, vjust  = 3)) +
  ylab("Depth (m)")
# plot.ALOHAS <-
#   f_depth_plots(posts.ALOHAS) + ggtitle("ALOHA Summer")
# plot.ALOHAW <-
#   f_depth_plots(posts.ALOHAW) + ggtitle("ALOHA Winter")
# plot.8N <-
#   f_depth_plots(posts.8N) + ggtitle("8 N")
# plot.5N <-
#   f_depth_plots(posts.5N) + ggtitle("5 N")

plot.PAP.trophic <-
  trophic_depth_plots(posts.PAP) + ggtitle("Trophic Parameters")
plot.OSP.trophic <-
  trophic_depth_plots(posts.OSP) + ggtitle("Trophic Parameters")
# plot.ALOHAS <-
#   trophic_depth_plots(posts.ALOHAS) + ggtitle("ALOHA Summer")
# plot.ALOHAW <-
#   trophic_depth_plots(posts.ALOHAW) + ggtitle("ALOHA Winter")
# plot.8N <-
#   trophic_depth_plots(posts.8N) + ggtitle("8 N")
# plot.5N <-
#   trophic_depth_plots(posts.5N) + ggtitle("5 N")

plot.blank <-
  ggplot+geom_blank()+theme_bw()+theme(panel.border=element_blank())

ggarrange(
  
  ggplot() + geom_blank() + ggtitle("North Atlantic") +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold")),
  
  ggarrange(plot.PAP.f,    plot.PAP.trophic,
            # plot.ALOHAS, plot.ALOHAW,
            # plot.5N,     plot.8N,
            ncol = 2, nrow=1,
            common.legend = TRUE, legend="none"),
  
  ggplot() + geom_blank(),
  
  ggplot() + geom_blank() + ggtitle("Station Papa") +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold")),
  
  ggarrange(plot.OSP.f,    plot.OSP.trophic,
            # plot.ALOHAS, plot.ALOHAW,
            # plot.5N,     plot.8N,
            ncol = 2, nrow=1,
            common.legend = TRUE, legend="bottom"),
  
  nrow = 5, heights = c(0.1,1,0.05,0.1,1.5)
)
```

```{r OSP-PAP_biplots, DPI = 300, fig.width=5, fig.height=5, cache=FALSE}

sizes <- c("0.06-0.2 mm", "0.2-0.5 mm", "1-2 mm", "2-5 mm", ">5 mm",
           "Oithonid", "Harpactacoid", "Pleuromamma", "Peuromamma", "Ostracod", "Oncaeid", "Gaussia", "Chaetognath","Radiolarian", "Metridia", "Neocalanus","Gaetanus")
f.mode <-
  subset(
  bind_rows(
    posts.5N$f$mode[c(variables,"Surface","Large","Small")],
    posts.8N$f$mode[c(variables,"Surface","Large","Small")],
    posts.ALOHAS$f$mode[c(variables,"Surface","Large","Small")],
    posts.ALOHAW$f$mode[c(variables,"Surface","Large","Small")],
    posts.OSP$f$mode[c(variables,"Surface","Large","Small")],
    posts.PAP$f$mode[c(variables,"Surface","Large","Small")]),
  Size != "0.06-0.2 mm" & Size != "0.2-0.5 mm" & Size != "1-2 mm" & Size != "2-5 mm" & Size != ">5 mm")# & Location == i)
f.mode.long <-
  melt(f.mode,
       id.vars = c(variables),
       variable.name = "Source",
       value.name = "f")
f.mode.long$Size <- factor(f.mode.long$Size, levels = sizes)
f.HDI95 <-
  subset(
    bind_rows(
    posts.5N$f$HDI95[c(variables,"Surface","Large","Small")],
    posts.8N$f$HDI95[c(variables,"Surface","Large","Small")],
    posts.ALOHAS$f$HDI95[c(variables,"Surface","Large","Small")],
    posts.ALOHAW$f$HDI95[c(variables,"Surface","Large","Small")],
    posts.OSP$f$HDI95[c(variables,"Surface","Large","Small")],
    posts.PAP$f$HDI95[c(variables,"Surface","Large","Small")]),
  Size != "0.06-0.2 mm" & Size != "0.2-0.5 mm" & Size != "1-2 mm" & Size != "2-5 mm" & Size != ">5 mm")# & Location == i)
f.HDI95.long <-
  melt(f.HDI95,
       id.vars = c(variables),
       variable.name = "Source",
       value.name = "f")
f.HDI95.long$Size <- factor(f.HDI95.long$Size, levels = sizes)
f.HDI90 <-
  subset(
    bind_rows(
    posts.5N$f$HDI90[c(variables,"Surface","Large","Small")],
    posts.8N$f$HDI90[c(variables,"Surface","Large","Small")],
    posts.ALOHAS$f$HDI90[c(variables,"Surface","Large","Small")],
    posts.ALOHAW$f$HDI90[c(variables,"Surface","Large","Small")],
    posts.OSP$f$HDI90[c(variables,"Surface","Large","Small")],
    posts.PAP$f$HDI90[c(variables,"Surface","Large","Small")]),
  Size != "0.06-0.2 mm" & Size != "0.2-0.5 mm" & Size != "1-2 mm" & Size != "2-5 mm" & Size != ">5 mm")# & Location == i)
f.HDI90.long <-
  melt(f.HDI90,
       id.vars = c(variables),
       variable.name = "Source",
       value.name = "f")
f.HDI90.long$Size <- factor(f.HDI90.long$Size, levels = sizes)
f.HDI75 <-
  subset(
    bind_rows(
    posts.5N$f$HDI75[c(variables,"Surface","Large","Small")],
    posts.8N$f$HDI75[c(variables,"Surface","Large","Small")],
    posts.ALOHAS$f$HDI75[c(variables,"Surface","Large","Small")],
    posts.ALOHAW$f$HDI75[c(variables,"Surface","Large","Small")],
    posts.OSP$f$HDI75[c(variables,"Surface","Large","Small")],
    posts.PAP$f$HDI75[c(variables,"Surface","Large","Small")]),
  Size != "0.06-0.2 mm" & Size != "0.2-0.5 mm" & Size != "1-2 mm" & Size != "2-5 mm" & Size != ">5 mm")# & Location == i)
f.HDI75.long <-
  melt(f.HDI75,
       id.vars = c(variables),
       variable.name = "Source",
       value.name = "f")
f.HDI75.long$Size <- factor(f.HDI75.long$Size, levels = sizes)
f.HDI50 <-
  subset(
    bind_rows(
    posts.5N$f$HDI50[c(variables,"Surface","Large","Small")],
    posts.8N$f$HDI50[c(variables,"Surface","Large","Small")],
    posts.ALOHAS$f$HDI50[c(variables,"Surface","Large","Small")],
    posts.ALOHAW$f$HDI50[c(variables,"Surface","Large","Small")],
    posts.OSP$f$HDI50[c(variables,"Surface","Large","Small")],
    posts.PAP$f$HDI50[c(variables,"Surface","Large","Small")]),
  Size != "0.06-0.2 mm" & Size != "0.2-0.5 mm" & Size != "1-2 mm" & Size != "2-5 mm" & Size != ">5 mm")# & Location == i)
f.HDI50.long <-
  melt(f.HDI50,
       id.vars = c(variables),
       variable.name = "Source",
       value.name = "f")
f.HDI50.long$Size <- factor(f.HDI50.long$Size, levels = sizes)

# Appending columns with the min and max of the 95% HDI to the mode data frame.
f.mode.long$min95 <- f.HDI95.long$f[seq(1,nrow(f.HDI95.long),2)]
f.mode.long$max95 <- f.HDI95.long$f[seq(2,nrow(f.HDI95.long),2)]

trophic.mode <-
  subset(
  bind_rows(
    posts.5N$trophic$mode[c(variables,"PTS","MTS","FWL")],
    posts.8N$trophic$mode[c(variables,"PTS","MTS","FWL")],
    posts.ALOHAS$trophic$mode[c(variables,"PTS","MTS","FWL")],
    posts.ALOHAW$trophic$mode[c(variables,"PTS","MTS","FWL")],
    posts.OSP$trophic$mode[c(variables,"PTS","MTS","FWL")],
    posts.PAP$trophic$mode[c(variables,"PTS","MTS","FWL")]),
  Size != "0.06-0.2 mm" & Size != "0.2-0.5 mm" & Size != "1-2 mm" & Size != "2-5 mm" & Size != ">5 mm")# & Location == i)
trophic.mode.long <-
  melt(trophic.mode,
       id.vars = c(variables),
       variable.name = "Source",
       value.name = "value")
trophic.mode.long$Size <- factor(trophic.mode.long$Size, levels = sizes)
trophic.HDI95 <-
  subset(
    bind_rows(
    posts.5N$trophic$HDI95[c(variables,"PTS","MTS","FWL")],
    posts.8N$trophic$HDI95[c(variables,"PTS","MTS","FWL")],
    posts.ALOHAS$trophic$HDI95[c(variables,"PTS","MTS","FWL")],
    posts.ALOHAW$trophic$HDI95[c(variables,"PTS","MTS","FWL")],
    posts.OSP$trophic$HDI95[c(variables,"PTS","MTS","FWL")],
    posts.PAP$trophic$HDI95[c(variables,"PTS","MTS","FWL")]),
  Size != "0.06-0.2 mm" & Size != "0.2-0.5 mm" & Size != "1-2 mm" & Size != "2-5 mm" & Size != ">5 mm")# & Location == i)
trophic.HDI95.long <-
  melt(trophic.HDI95,
       id.vars = c(variables),
       variable.name = "Source",
       value.name = "value")
trophic.HDI95.long$Size <- factor(trophic.HDI95.long$Size, levels = sizes)
trophic.HDI90 <-
  subset(
    bind_rows(
    posts.5N$trophic$HDI90[c(variables,"PTS","MTS","FWL")],
    posts.8N$trophic$HDI90[c(variables,"PTS","MTS","FWL")],
    posts.ALOHAS$trophic$HDI90[c(variables,"PTS","MTS","FWL")],
    posts.ALOHAW$trophic$HDI90[c(variables,"PTS","MTS","FWL")],
    posts.OSP$trophic$HDI90[c(variables,"PTS","MTS","FWL")],
    posts.PAP$trophic$HDI90[c(variables,"PTS","MTS","FWL")]),
  Size != "0.06-0.2 mm" & Size != "0.2-0.5 mm" & Size != "1-2 mm" & Size != "2-5 mm" & Size != ">5 mm")# & Location == i)
trophic.HDI90.long <-
  melt(trophic.HDI90,
       id.vars = c(variables),
       variable.name = "Source",
       value.name = "value")
trophic.HDI90.long$Size <- factor(trophic.HDI90.long$Size, levels = sizes)
trophic.HDI75 <-
  subset(
    bind_rows(
    posts.5N$trophic$HDI75[c(variables,"PTS","MTS","FWL")],
    posts.8N$trophic$HDI75[c(variables,"PTS","MTS","FWL")],
    posts.ALOHAS$trophic$HDI75[c(variables,"PTS","MTS","FWL")],
    posts.ALOHAW$trophic$HDI75[c(variables,"PTS","MTS","FWL")],
    posts.OSP$trophic$HDI75[c(variables,"PTS","MTS","FWL")],
    posts.PAP$trophic$HDI75[c(variables,"PTS","MTS","FWL")]),
  Size != "0.06-0.2 mm" & Size != "0.2-0.5 mm" & Size != "1-2 mm" & Size != "2-5 mm" & Size != ">5 mm")# & Location == i)
trophic.HDI75.long <-
  melt(trophic.HDI75,
       id.vars = c(variables),
       variable.name = "Source",
       value.name = "value")
trophic.HDI75.long$Size <- factor(trophic.HDI75.long$Size, levels = sizes)
trophic.HDI50 <-
  subset(
    bind_rows(
    posts.5N$trophic$HDI50[c(variables,"PTS","MTS","FWL")],
    posts.8N$trophic$HDI50[c(variables,"PTS","MTS","FWL")],
    posts.ALOHAS$trophic$HDI50[c(variables,"PTS","MTS","FWL")],
    posts.ALOHAW$trophic$HDI50[c(variables,"PTS","MTS","FWL")],
    posts.OSP$trophic$HDI50[c(variables,"PTS","MTS","FWL")],
    posts.PAP$trophic$HDI50[c(variables,"PTS","MTS","FWL")]),
  Size != "0.06-0.2 mm" & Size != "0.2-0.5 mm" & Size != "1-2 mm" & Size != "2-5 mm" & Size != ">5 mm")# & Location == i)
trophic.HDI50.long <-
  melt(trophic.HDI50,
       id.vars = c(variables),
       variable.name = "Source",
       value.name = "value")
trophic.HDI50.long$Size <- factor(trophic.HDI50.long$Size, levels = sizes)

# Appending columns with the min and max of the 95% HDI to the mode data frame.
trophic.mode.long$min95 <- trophic.HDI95.long$trophic[seq(1,nrow(trophic.HDI95.long),2)]
trophic.mode.long$max95 <- trophic.HDI95.long$trophic[seq(2,nrow(trophic.HDI95.long),2)]

biplot.data <- f.mode[1:7]
biplot.data$Surface <- f.mode$Surface
biplot.data$SurfaceMin <- f.HDI95$Surface[seq(1,nrow(f.HDI95),2)]
biplot.data$SurfaceMax <- f.HDI95$Surface[seq(2,nrow(f.HDI95),2)]
biplot.data$FWL <- trophic.mode$FWL
biplot.data$FWLMin <- trophic.HDI95$FWL[seq(1,nrow(trophic.HDI95),2)]
biplot.data$FWLMax <- trophic.HDI95$FWL[seq(2,nrow(trophic.HDI95),2)]

biplot.data$Clade <- NA
biplot.data[which(
  biplot.data$Size == "Chaetognath" | biplot.data$Size == "Gaetanus"
  ),]$Clade <- "Carnivore"
biplot.data[which(
  biplot.data$Size == "Oncaeid" | biplot.data$Size == "Oithonid"  
  ),]$Clade <- "Particle Feeder"
biplot.data[which(
  biplot.data$Size == "Metridia" | biplot.data$Size == "Pleuromamma" | biplot.data$Size == "Harpactacoid"
  ),]$Clade <- "Vertical Migrator"

biplot.data <-
  subset(biplot.data, !is.na(Clade))


ggplot(
  data = subset(biplot.data),
  aes(x = Surface, xmax = SurfaceMax, xmin = SurfaceMin,
      y = FWL, ymax = FWLMax, ymin = FWLMin,
      color = Clade, fill = Clade)
  ) +
  geom_point() +
  stat_ellipse(type = "t", level = 0.95, geom = "polygon", alpha = 0.2) +
  labs(color = "Feeding Clade", fill = "Feeding Clade")

```

# Corrected $\dNSAA$ profiles {-}

Let's also try plotting depth profiles of the average $\dN$ values of all SAAs (Ser, Gly, Phe, Lys) after being corrected for trophic discrimination, alongside those of traps and particles.

```{r SAA-depth_profiles, warning=FALSE, dpi=300, fig.width=8.5, fig.height=5}

sizes <- c("0.06-0.2 mm", "0.2-0.5 mm", "1-2 mm", "2-5 mm", ">5 mm",
           "Oithonid", "Harpactacoid", "Pleuromamma", "Peuromamma", "Ostracod", "Oncaeid", "Gaussia", "Chaetognath","Radiolarian", "Metridia", "Neocalanus","Gaetanus")
variables.temp <- colnames(posts$base$mode)[1:9]
base.mode <- 
  subset(
  bind_rows(
    posts.5N$base$mode,
    posts.8N$base$mode,
    posts.ALOHAS$base$mode,
    posts.ALOHAW$base$mode,
    posts.OSP$base$mode,
    posts.PAP$base$mode),
  Size != "0.06-0.2 mm")# & Location == i)
base.mode$SAA <- rowMeans(cbind(base.mode$d15NSer, base.mode$d15NGly, base.mode$d15NPhe, base.mode$d15NLys), na.rm = TRUE)
base.mode.long <-
  melt(base.mode,
       id.vars = c(variables.temp),
       variable.name = "AA",
       value.name = "d15N")
base.mode.long$Size <- factor(base.mode.long$Size, levels = sizes)
base.HDI95 <- 
  subset(
    bind_rows(
    posts.5N$base$HDI95,
    posts.8N$base$HDI95,
    posts.ALOHAS$base$HDI95,
    posts.ALOHAW$base$HDI95,
    posts.OSP$base$HDI95,
    posts.PAP$base$HDI95),
  Size != "0.06-0.2 mm")# & Location == i)
base.HDI95$SAA <- rowMeans(cbind(base.HDI95$d15NSer, base.HDI95$d15NGly, base.HDI95$d15NPhe, base.HDI95$d15NLys), na.rm = TRUE)
base.HDI95.long <-
  melt(base.HDI95,
       id.vars = c(variables.temp),
       variable.name = "AA",
       value.name = "d15N")
base.HDI95.long$Size <- factor(base.HDI95.long$Size, levels = sizes)
base.HDI90 <- 
  subset(
    bind_rows(
    posts.5N$base$HDI90,
    posts.8N$base$HDI90,
    posts.ALOHAS$base$HDI90,
    posts.ALOHAW$base$HDI90,
    posts.OSP$base$HDI90,
    posts.PAP$base$HDI90),
  Size != "0.06-0.2 mm")# & Location == i)
base.HDI90$SAA <- rowMeans(cbind(base.HDI90$d15NSer, base.HDI90$d15NGly, base.HDI90$d15NPhe, base.HDI90$d15NLys), na.rm = TRUE)
base.HDI90.long <-
  melt(base.HDI90,
       id.vars = c(variables.temp),
       variable.name = "AA",
       value.name = "d15N")
base.HDI90.long$Size <- factor(base.HDI90.long$Size, levels = sizes)
base.HDI75 <- 
  subset(
    bind_rows(
    posts.5N$base$HDI75,
    posts.8N$base$HDI75,
    posts.ALOHAS$base$HDI75,
    posts.ALOHAW$base$HDI75,
    posts.OSP$base$HDI75,
    posts.PAP$base$HDI75),
  Size != "0.06-0.2 mm")# & Location == i)
base.HDI75$SAA <- rowMeans(cbind(base.HDI75$d15NSer, base.HDI75$d15NGly, base.HDI75$d15NPhe, base.HDI75$d15NLys), na.rm = TRUE)
base.HDI75.long <-
  melt(base.HDI75,
       id.vars = c(variables.temp),
       variable.name = "AA",
       value.name = "d15N")
base.HDI75.long$Size <- factor(base.HDI75.long$Size, levels = sizes)
base.HDI50 <- 
  subset(
    bind_rows(
    posts.5N$base$HDI50,
    posts.8N$base$HDI50,
    posts.ALOHAS$base$HDI50,
    posts.ALOHAW$base$HDI50,
    posts.OSP$base$HDI50,
    posts.PAP$base$HDI50),
  Size != "0.06-0.2 mm")# & Location == i)
base.HDI50$SAA <- rowMeans(cbind(base.HDI50$d15NSer, base.HDI50$d15NGly, base.HDI50$d15NPhe, base.HDI50$d15NLys), na.rm = TRUE)
base.HDI50.long <-
  melt(base.HDI50,
       id.vars = c(variables.temp),
       variable.name = "AA",
       value.name = "d15N")
base.HDI50.long$Size <- factor(base.HDI50.long$Size, levels = sizes)

# Appending columns with the min and max of the 95% HDI to the mode data frame.
base.mode.long$min95 <- base.HDI95.long$f[seq(1,nrow(base.HDI95.long),2)]
base.mode.long$max95 <- base.HDI95.long$f[seq(2,nrow(base.HDI95.long),2)]

data.all$SAA <- rowMeans(cbind(data.all$d15NSer, data.all$d15NGly, data.all$d15NPhe, data.all$d15NLys), na.rm = TRUE)
    
  data.plots.traps <- subset(data.all, Event == "Trap Deployment")
  data.plots.parts <- subset(data.all, Event == "Pump Cast" & Sizecat != "Surface")
  data.plots.zoops <- subset(base.mode, Size == "0.2-0.5 mm" | Size == "1-2 mm" | Size == "2-5 mm" | Size == ">5 mm")
  maxdepth <-
    round(max(data.plots.zoops$Depth)+50, -2)
  
  ggplot()+
    geom_smooth( # Particles
      data = data.plots.parts,
      aes(x = SAA, y = Depth, color = Sizecat, fill = Sizecat, group = Sizecat), 
      orientation = "y", level=0.75, span=1.2)+
    geom_point(
      data = data.plots.parts,
      aes(x = SAA, y = Depth, color = Sizecat, fill = Sizecat, group = Sizecat), 
      size=1, shape=21, alpha=0.7)+
    geom_point( # Zooplankton
      data = data.plots.zoops,
      aes(x = SAA, y = Depth, shape = Size),
      color = "black", size = 1.5, stroke = 0.95)+
    # geom_path(
    #   data = data.plots.zoops,
    #   aes(x = SAA, y = Depth, group = Size, inherit = FALSE),
    #   color = "black", size = 1, linetype = "solid", alpha = 0.5)+
    geom_smooth(
      data = data.plots.zoops,
      aes(x = SAA, y = Depth),
      orientation = "y", level=0.95, span=0.9, color = "black", se = FALSE)+
    # geom_errorbarh(
    #   data = data.plots.zoops,
    #   aes(x = SAA, y = Depth, xmin = min95, xmax = max95), 
    #   height = 1, size = 0.5, linetype = 1, color = 'black')+
    geom_point( # Sediment Traps
      data = data.plots.traps,
      aes(x = SAA, y = Depth, color = Sizecat, fill = Sizecat),
      size = 1.5, stroke = 0.75, shape = 6)+
    # geom_errorbarh(
    #   data = data.plots.traps,
    #   aes(x = SAA, y = Depth, xmin = min95, xmax = max95), 
    #   height = 1, size = 0.5, linetype = 1, color = 'black')+
    scale_y_reverse() + ylab('Depth (m)') +
    scale_x_continuous(minor_breaks = seq(-8,16,2), breaks = seq(-8,16,4))+ 
    xlab(expression(delta^{15}*"N"[SAA]*" (\u2030)")) + 
    labs(color = "Particles", fill="Particles", shape = "Zooplankton")+
    theme_light()+theme(strip.text.x = element_text(size = 8))+ 
    coord_cartesian(expand = FALSE, ylim = c(maxdepth,0))+
    facet_wrap(~ interaction(Location), nrow = 2, scales = "free", dir="v")+
    scale_color_discrete(type = c("#F8766D", "#00BA38", "#619CFF","black"))+
    scale_fill_discrete(type = c("#F8766D", "#00BA38", "#619CFF","white"))
# }

```

# Are trophic parameters related to f(surface)? {-}

```{r trophic-vs-f, dpi = 300, fig.width = 5, fig.asp = 0.6}
trophic.mode <- 
  bind_rows(
    posts.5N$trophic$mode[c(variables,"PTS","MTS","FWL")],
    posts.8N$trophic$mode[c(variables,"PTS","MTS","FWL")],
    posts.ALOHAS$trophic$mode[c(variables,"PTS","MTS","FWL")],
    posts.ALOHAW$trophic$mode[c(variables,"PTS","MTS","FWL")],
    posts.OSP$trophic$mode[c(variables,"PTS","MTS","FWL")],
    posts.PAP$trophic$mode[c(variables,"PTS","MTS","FWL")])
  # Size != "0.06-0.2 mm")
trophic.mode$baseTP <- (base.mode$d15NAla - base.mode$d15NPhe - 3.2) / 6.3 + 1
trophic.mode <- 
  subset(trophic.mode, Size == "0.2-0.5 mm" | Size == "1-2 mm" | Size == "2-5 mm" | Size == ">5 mm")

f.mode <-
  subset(
  bind_rows(
    posts.5N$f$mode[c(variables,"Surface","Large","Small")],
    posts.8N$f$mode[c(variables,"Surface","Large","Small")],
    posts.ALOHAS$f$mode[c(variables,"Surface","Large","Small")],
    posts.ALOHAW$f$mode[c(variables,"Surface","Large","Small")],
    posts.OSP$f$mode[c(variables,"Surface","Large","Small")],
    posts.PAP$f$mode[c(variables,"Surface","Large","Small")]),
  # Size != "0.06-0.2 mm")
  Size == "0.2-0.5 mm" | Size == "1-2 mm" | Size == "2-5 mm" | Size == ">5 mm")

posterior.modes <- cbind(trophic.mode, f.mode[c("Surface","Large","Small")])

ggplot(
  data = subset(posterior.modes, Depth > 200),
  aes(x = Surface, y = FWL, color = -Depth, fill = -Depth))+
  geom_smooth(
    method = "lm", se = FALSE, lty = 2)+
  geom_smooth(
    method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "black", fill = "grey50")+
  geom_point(
    size = 3, shape = 21, color = "black")

LM <- lm(
  FWL ~ Surface + Depth,
  data = posterior.modes)
summary(LM)
anova(LM)
plot(allEffects(LM))

ggplot(
  data = subset(posterior.modes, Depth > 200),
  aes(x = Surface, y = baseTP, color = -Depth, fill = -Depth))+
  geom_smooth(
    method = "lm", se = FALSE, lty = 2)+
  geom_smooth(
    method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "black", fill = "grey50")+
  geom_point(
    size = 3, shape = 21, color = "black")
```


# Comparison with oceanographic parameters - all samples {-}

Now that we've established values for mixing coefficients and trophic coefficients for zooplankton across these various locations, we'd like to examine some potential basin-scale trends in these parameters. Here are the variables we think might reasonably predict the importance of different organic matter sources to the food web.

**Depth: ** distance from the surface should influence the importance of surface organic matter to the food web. We also might expect that smaller particles might become a more important food source at depth, though this might not be a linear effect.

**NPP: ** The amount of NPP occurring in a given location should dictate the amount of sinking material available to MPZ communities. On the other hand, more NPP is also generally correlateed with more DVM.

**EXPORT: ** The amount of POM leaving the euphotic zone should also impact thee importance of sinking particles to the MPZ community.

**DVM Biomass: ** The quantity of animals doing DVM should directly influence the availability of surface POM to the MPZ community.

**Export Ratio: ** The ratio of NPP to EXPORT is related to how efficiently the EZ community consumes primary production. A low export ratio would indicate high NPP with low export, which should favor DVM as a strategy for bringing carbon to depth. A high export ratio should favor a sit and wait strategy of consuming sinking POM within the MPZ.

Let's do some quick regressions to assess these hypotheses. Of course, this exercise has been done before by Romero-romero et al. (2020). We are simply repeating it with revised mixing model results.

Since we are essentially regressing compositional data, a binomial or quasibinomial distribution would probably be best.

```{r}
data.romero <- as.data.frame(read_excel("Data/Romero-Romero2020.xlsx", sheet = "Romero-Romero2020(wPAP)" ))

f.mode <- 
  subset(
    bind_rows(
    posts.5N$f$mode[c(variables,"Surface","Large","Small")],
    posts.8N$f$mode[c(variables,"Surface","Large","Small")],
    posts.ALOHAS$f$mode[c(variables,"Surface","Large","Small")],
    posts.ALOHAW$f$mode[c(variables,"Surface","Large","Small")],
    posts.OSP$f$mode[c(variables,"Surface","Large","Small")],
    posts.PAP$f$mode[c(variables,"Surface","Large","Small")]),
    Size == "0.2-0.5 mm" | Size == "1-2 mm" | Size == "2-5 mm" | Size == ">5 mm"
    # Size == "0.2-0.5 mm" | 
    # Size == "1-2 mm"
    )

# WORKING ON POOLING SIZE CLASSES WITHIN EACH DEPTH AT EACH LOCATION
f.mode.pooled <- aggregate(
  x = cbind(Surface, Large, Small) ~ Location + Epoch + Event + Depth + Type,
  data = f.mode,
  FUN = mean
  )


trophic.mode <- 
  subset(
  bind_rows(
    posts.5N$trophic$mode[c(variables,"PTS","MTS","FWL")],
    posts.8N$trophic$mode[c(variables,"PTS","MTS","FWL")],
    posts.ALOHAS$trophic$mode[c(variables,"PTS","MTS","FWL")],
    posts.ALOHAW$trophic$mode[c(variables,"PTS","MTS","FWL")],
    posts.OSP$trophic$mode[c(variables,"PTS","MTS","FWL")],
    posts.PAP$trophic$mode[c(variables,"PTS","MTS","FWL")]),
  Size != "0.06-0.2 mm")# & Location == i)
# Let's calculate TP at the base of the food web
base.mode <- 
  subset(
  bind_rows(
    posts.5N$base$mode[c(variables,"d15NAla","d15NPhe")],
    posts.8N$base$mode[c(variables,"d15NAla","d15NPhe")],
    posts.ALOHAS$base$mode[c(variables,"d15NAla","d15NPhe")],
    posts.ALOHAW$base$mode[c(variables,"d15NAla","d15NPhe")],
    posts.OSP$base$mode[c(variables,"d15NAla","d15NPhe")],
    posts.PAP$base$mode[c(variables,"d15NAla","d15NPhe")]),
  Size != "0.06-0.2 mm")# & Location == i)
trophic.mode$baseTP <- (base.mode$d15NAla - base.mode$d15NPhe - 3.2) / 6.3 + 1
trophic.mode$Size <- factor(trophic.mode$Size, levels = sizes)

trophic.mode.mean <- aggregate(
  x = cbind(FWL,baseTP) ~ Depth + Location,
  data = subset(trophic.mode,
                Size == "0.2-0.5 mm" | Size == "1-2 mm" | 
                  Size == "2-5 mm" | Size == ">5 mm"),
  FUN = "mean"
)
```

## Depth {-}

We'll look at trends across depth at each location. Looking at the depth profile above, we see that if there is a depth dependence in mixing coefficients its is probably non-linear. We'll start, then, by taking the square root transform of depth.

First we will do this on all the data across all locations.

```{r Depth, fig.width=8, fig.asp=0.4}

print("===== SURFACE =====")
plot1 <- ggplot(
  data = f.mode.pooled,
  aes(x = Depth, y = Surface, color = Location, fill = Location))+
  geom_smooth(
    method = "glm", method.args = list(family = quasibinomial()),
    se = TRUE, alpha = 0.2, color = "black", fill = "grey50")+
  geom_point(
    size = 3, shape = 21, color = "black")+
  ggtitle("Surface")
MOD1 <- glm(Surface ~ Depth,
         data = f.mode.pooled,
         family = quasibinomial())
print("----- SUMMARY -----")
summary(MOD1)
print("----- ANOVA -----")
anova(MOD1)
# plot(allEffects(MOD1))

print("===== LARGE =====")
plot2 <- ggplot(
  data = f.mode.pooled,
  aes(x = Depth, y = Large, color = Location, fill = Location))+
  geom_smooth(
    method = "glm", method.args = list(family = quasibinomial()),
    se = TRUE, alpha = 0.2, color = "black", fill = "grey50")+
  geom_point(
    size = 3, shape = 21, color = "black")+
  ggtitle("Large")
MOD2 <- glm(Large ~ Depth,
         data = f.mode.pooled,
         family = quasibinomial()
)
print("----- SUMMARY -----")
summary(MOD2)
print("----- ANOVA -----")
anova(MOD2)
# plot(allEffects(MOD2))

print("===== SMALL =====")
plot3 <- ggplot(
  data = f.mode.pooled,
  aes(x = Depth, y = Small, color = Location, fill = Location))+
  geom_smooth(
    method = "glm", method.args = list(family = quasibinomial()),
    se = TRUE, alpha = 0.2, color = "black", fill = "grey50")+
  geom_point(
    size = 3, shape = 21, color = "black")+
  ggtitle("Small")
MOD3 <- glm(Small ~ Depth,
         data = f.mode.pooled,
         family = quasibinomial()
)
print("----- SUMMARY -----")
summary(MOD3)
print("----- ANOVA -----")
anova(MOD3)
# plot(allEffects(MOD3))

ggarrange(
  plot1, plot2, plot3,
  nrow = 1, common.legend = TRUE, legend = "bottom"
)
```

There appears to be a statistically significant relationship between f(surface) and Depth. This is true for the aggregated data (p = `r anova(MOD1)[2,6]`), but the fit is poor because of high values for f(surface) at deeper depths at ALOHA. There is also true for f(small) (p = `r anova(MOD3)[2,6]`)

Now we'll specify location as a second predictor.

```{r Depth-Location, fig.width=8, fig.height=6, eval=FALSE}

print("===== SURFACE =====")

plot1 <- ggplot(
  data = f.mode,
  aes(x = Depth, y = Surface, color = Size, fill = Size))+
  geom_smooth(
    method = "glm", method.args = list(family = quasibinomial()),
    se = TRUE, alpha = 0.2)+
  geom_point(
    size = 3, shape = 21, color = "black")+
  facet_wrap(~ Location, nrow=1)+
  ylim(0,1)+
  ggtitle("Surface")
MOD1 <- glm(Surface ~ Depth + Location + Size,
         data = f.mode,
         family = quasibinomial())
print("----- SUMMARY -----")
summary(MOD1)
print("----- ANOVA -----")
anova(MOD1)
plot(allEffects(MOD1))

print("===== LARGE =====")
plot2 <- ggplot(
  data = f.mode,
  aes(x = Depth, y = Large, color = Size, fill = Size))+
  geom_smooth(
    method = "glm", method.args = list(family = quasibinomial()),
    se = TRUE, alpha = 0.2)+
  geom_point(
    size = 3, shape = 21, color = "black")+
  facet_wrap(~ Location, nrow=1)+
  ylim(0,1)+
  ggtitle("Large")
MOD2 <- glm(Large ~ Depth + Location + Size,
         data = f.mode,
         family = quasibinomial())
print("----- SUMMARY -----")
summary(MOD2)
print("----- ANOVA -----")
anova(MOD2)
plot(allEffects(MOD2))

print("===== SMALL =====")
plot3 <- ggplot(
  data = f.mode,
  aes(x = Depth, y = Small, color = Size, fill = Size))+
  geom_smooth(
    method = "glm", method.args = list(family = quasibinomial()),
    se = TRUE, alpha = 0.2)+
  geom_point(
    size = 3, shape = 21, color = "black")+
  facet_wrap(~ Location, nrow=1)+
  ylim(0,1)+
  ggtitle("Small")
MOD3 <- glm(Small ~ Depth + Location + Size,
         data = f.mode,
         family = quasibinomial())
print("----- SUMMARY -----")
summary(MOD3)
print("----- ANOVA -----")
anova(MOD3)
plot(allEffects(MOD3))

ggarrange(
  plot1, plot2, plot3,
  nrow = 3, common.legend = TRUE, legend = "bottom"
)
```

The fit with f(surface) is much stronger when location is included as a predictor in the model (p = `r anova(MOD1)[2,6]`). Location is a also a significant predictor (p = `r anova(MOD1)[3,6]`).

This suggests that in general surface particles become less important at deeper depths, but that the nature of this relationship is location specific. Let's figure out what is driving these trends across space by looking using site-specific oceanographic variables as predictors. We will need to include depth as a predictor in these models as well, though we may choose to do this by just subsetting the data within specific depth bins.

## NPP {-}

```{r incorportating-NPP-data}
locs <- unique(f.mode$Location)
for (i in locs) {
  f.mode$NPP[which(f.mode$Location == i)] <- 
    data.romero$`NPP (best)`[which(data.romero$Site == i)]
}
locs <- unique(f.mode.pooled$Location)
f.mode.pooled$NPP <- NA
for (i in locs) {
  f.mode.pooled$NPP[which(f.mode.pooled$Location == i)] <-
    data.romero$`NPP (best)`[which(data.romero$Site == i)]
}

locs <- unique(f.mode.pooled$Location)
trophic.mode.mean$NPP <- NA
for (i in locs) {
  trophic.mode.mean$NPP[which(trophic.mode.mean$Location == i)] <-
    data.romero$`NPP (best)`[which(data.romero$Site == i)]
}
```

First we'll try just doing a glm with NPP as a predictor for f(surface).

```{r NPP, fig.width=8, fig.asp=0.4}

print("===== SURFACE =====")

plot1 <- ggplot(
  data = f.mode.pooled,
  aes(x = NPP, y = Surface, color = Location, fill = Location))+
  geom_smooth(
    data = subset(f.mode.pooled, Location != "PAP"),
    method = "glm", method.args = list(family = quasibinomial()),
    se = TRUE, color = "black", fill="grey")+
  geom_point(
    size = 3, shape = 21, color = "black")+
  ylim(0,1)+
  ggtitle("Surface")
MOD1 <- glm(Surface ~ NPP,
         data = f.mode,
         family = quasibinomial())
print("----- SUMMARY -----")
summary(MOD1)
print("----- ANOVA -----")
anova(MOD1)
# plot(allEffects(MOD1))

print("===== LARGE =====")
plot2 <- ggplot(
  data = f.mode.pooled,
  aes(x = NPP, y = Large, color = Location, fill = Location))+
  geom_smooth(
    data = subset(f.mode.pooled, Location != "PAP"),
    method = "glm", method.args = list(family = quasibinomial()),
    se = TRUE, color = "black", fill="grey")+
  geom_point(
    size = 3, shape = 21, color = "black")+
  ylim(0,1)+
  ggtitle("Large")
MOD2 <- glm(Large ~ NPP,
         data = f.mode,
         family = quasibinomial())
print("----- SUMMARY -----")
summary(MOD2)
print("----- ANOVA -----")
anova(MOD2)
# plot(allEffects(MOD2))

print("===== SMALL =====")
plot3 <- ggplot(
  data = f.mode.pooled,
  aes(x = NPP, y = Small, color = Location, fill = Location))+
  geom_smooth(
    data = subset(f.mode.pooled, Location != "PAP"),
    method = "glm", method.args = list(family = quasibinomial()),
    se = TRUE, color = "black", fill="grey")+
  geom_point(
    size = 3, shape = 21, color = "black")+
  ylim(0,1)+
  ggtitle("Small")
MOD3 <- glm(Small ~ NPP,
         data = f.mode,
         family = quasibinomial())
print("----- SUMMARY -----")
summary(MOD3)
print("----- ANOVA -----")
anova(MOD3)
# plot(allEffects(MOD3))

ggarrange(
  plot1, plot2, plot3,
  nrow = 1, common.legend = TRUE, legend = "bottom"
)

```

We also want to look and see if FWL or TP at the base of the foodweb are related to NPP.

```{r NPP-trophic, fig.width=5, fig.height=3, dpi=300}

ggarrange(

ggplot(
  data = subset(trophic.mode.mean, Depth>200),
  aes(x = NPP, y = baseTP, color = Location, fill = Location))+
  geom_smooth(
    data = subset(trophic.mode.mean),
    method = "lm", formula = y ~ poly(x, 2),
    se = TRUE, color = "black", fill="grey", )+
  geom_point(
    size = 3, shape = 21, color = "black")+
  theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(), 
        axis.text.x = element_blank())+
  ylim(1,2.5)
  ,

ggplot(
  data = subset(trophic.mode.mean, Depth>200),
  aes(x = NPP, y = FWL, color = Location, fill = Location))+
  geom_line(
    data = trophic.HDI95.long,
    aes(x = value, y = Depth, color = Size, group = interaction(Depth, Size)),
    alpha = 0.5, size = 1,
    position = position_dodgev(height = 100))+
  geom_line(
    data = trophic.HDI90.long,
    aes(x = value, y = Depth, color = Size, group = interaction(Depth, Size)),
    alpha = 0.7, size = 1.5,
    position = position_dodgev(height = 100))+
  geom_line(
    data = trophic.HDI75.long,
    aes(x = value, y = Depth, color = Size, group = interaction(Depth, Size)),
    alpha = 1, size = 2,
    position = position_dodgev(height = 100))+
  geom_line(
    data = trophic.HDI50.long,
    aes(x = value, y = Depth, color = Size, group = interaction(Depth, Size)),
    alpha = 1, size = 3,
    position = position_dodgev(height = 100))+
  geom_smooth(
    data = subset(trophic.mode.mean),
    method = "lm", formula = y ~ poly(x, 2),
    se = TRUE, color = "black", fill="grey", )+
  geom_point(
    size = 3, shape = 21, color = "black")+
  ylim(1,2.5),

nrow = 2,
heights = c(1,1.2),
legend = "right",
common.legend = TRUE
)

```

This is showing that NPP is a significant predictor of f(surface) (p = `r anova(MOD1)[2,6]`), f(large) (p = `r anova(MOD2)[2,6]`), and f(small) (p = `r anova(MOD3)[2,6]`). 

We know depth is a significant predictor so lets also include that in the GLM. We would include size, but we dont have enough representation of some sizes across sites so it doesn't come out as being significant.

```{r NPP-Depth, fig.width=8, fig.height=4}

MOD1 <- glm(Surface ~ NPP + Depth,
         data = f.mode,
         family = quasibinomial())
print("----- SUMMARY -----")
summary(MOD1)
print("----- ANOVA -----")
anova(MOD1)
plot(allEffects(MOD1))

print("===== LARGE =====")

MOD2 <- glm(Large ~ NPP + Depth,
         data = f.mode,
         family = quasibinomial())
print("----- SUMMARY -----")
summary(MOD2)
print("----- ANOVA -----")
anova(MOD2)
plot(allEffects(MOD2))

print("===== SMALL =====")
MOD3 <- glm(Small ~ NPP + Depth,
         data = f.mode,
         family = quasibinomial())
print("----- SUMMARY -----")
summary(MOD3)
print("----- ANOVA -----")
anova(MOD3)
plot(allEffects(MOD3))
```

NPP continues to be a significant predictor of f(surface) (p = `r anova(MOD1)[2,6]`), f(large) (p = `r anova(MOD2)[2,6]`), and f(small) (p = `r anova(MOD3)[2,6]`). 

It's hard to to plot that effect, and we may more interested in these relationships within specific depth strata, so let's make some plots just looking at the EZ (0-100 m), the upper MPZ (200-700 m), the lower MPZ (700-1500 m).

```{r DepthStrata-NPP, fig.width=8, fig.height=6, warning=FALSE}

f.mode$Strata <- factor("EZ", 
                        levels = c("EZ","MPZ upper","MPZ lower","Bathyl"))
f.mode$Strata[which(f.mode$Depth > 100)] <- "MPZ upper"
f.mode$Strata[which(f.mode$Depth > 700)] <- "MPZ lower"
# f.mode$Strata[which(f.mode$Depth > 1000)] <- "Bathyl"

f.mode.pooled$Strata <- factor("EZ", 
                        levels = c("EZ","MPZ upper","MPZ lower","Bathyl"))
f.mode.pooled$Strata[which(f.mode.pooled$Depth > 100)] <- "MPZ upper"
f.mode.pooled$Strata[which(f.mode.pooled$Depth > 700)] <- "MPZ lower"
# f.mode.pooled$Strata[which(f.mode.pooled$Depth > 1000)] <- "Bathyl"

print("===== SURFACE =====")

plot1 <- ggplot(
  data = f.mode.pooled,
  aes(x = NPP, y = Surface, color = Location, fill = Location))+
  geom_smooth(
    data = subset(f.mode.pooled, Location != "PAP"),
    method = "glm", method.args = list(family = quasibinomial()),
    se = TRUE, alpha = 0.2, color = "black", fill = "black")+
  geom_point(
    size = 3, shape = 21, color = "black")+
  facet_wrap(~ Strata, nrow = 3)+
  ylim(0,1)+
  ggtitle("Surface")
MOD1 <- glm(Surface ~ Depth + NPP,
         data = f.mode.pooled,
         family = quasibinomial())
print("----- SUMMARY -----")
summary(MOD1)
print("----- ANOVA -----")
anova(MOD1)
# plot(allEffects(MOD1))

print("===== LARGE =====")
plot2 <- ggplot(
  data = f.mode.pooled,
  aes(x = NPP, y = Large, color = Location, fill = Location))+
  geom_smooth(
    data = subset(f.mode.pooled, Location != "PAP"),
    method = "glm", method.args = list(family = quasibinomial()),
    se = TRUE, alpha = 0.2, color = "black", fill = "black")+
  geom_point(
    size = 3, shape = 21, color = "black")+
  facet_wrap(~ Strata, nrow = 3)+
  ylim(0,1)+
  ggtitle("Large")
MOD2 <- glm(Large ~ Depth + NPP,
         data = f.mode.pooled,
         family = quasibinomial())
print("----- SUMMARY -----")
summary(MOD2)
print("----- ANOVA -----")
anova(MOD2)
# plot(allEffects(MOD2))

print("===== SMALL =====")
plot3 <- ggplot(
  data = f.mode.pooled,
  aes(x = NPP, y = Small, color = Location, fill = Location))+
  geom_smooth(
    data = subset(f.mode.pooled, Location != "PAP"),
    method = "glm", method.args = list(family = quasibinomial()),
    se = TRUE, alpha = 0.2, color = "black", fill = "black")+
  geom_point(
    size = 3, shape = 21, color = "black")+
  facet_wrap(~ Strata, nrow = 3)+
  ylim(0,1)+
  ggtitle("Small")
MOD3 <- glm(Small ~ Depth + NPP,
         data = f.mode.pooled,
         family = quasibinomial())
print("----- SUMMARY -----")
summary(MOD3)
print("----- ANOVA -----")
anova(MOD3)
# plot(allEffects(MOD3))

ggarrange(
  plot1, plot2, plot3,
  nrow = 1, common.legend = TRUE, legend = "bottom"
)
```

Looking at the data this way we can see that the relationship with NPP is only strong within certain depth strata. Let's continue on to examine other predictors.

## Export {-}

Next we will look at particle export at the base of the euphotic zone. We define the base of the EZ here as the depth at which chl is 1% of its maximum. Export is estimated from Th-234 disequillibrium.

```{r incorportating-Export-data}
locs <- unique(f.mode$Location)
f.mode$Export <- as.numeric(NA)
for (i in locs) {
  f.mode$Export[which(f.mode$Location == i)] <- 
    as.numeric(data.romero$fluxC.PPZ.avg[which(data.romero$Site == i)])
}
locs <- unique(f.mode.pooled$Location)
f.mode.pooled$Export <- as.numeric(NA)
for (i in locs) {
  f.mode.pooled$Export[which(f.mode.pooled$Location == i)] <- 
    as.numeric(data.romero$fluxC.PPZ.avg[which(data.romero$Site == i)])
}
```

At this point we've established that location is an important secondary predictor to include. We will choose to implment depth strata as a discrete secondary predictor in regressions going forward.

```{r DepthStrata-Export, fig.width=8, fig.height=6, warning=FALSE}

f.mode$Strata <- factor("EZ", 
                        levels = c("EZ","MPZ upper","MPZ lower","Bathyl"))
f.mode$Strata[which(f.mode$Depth > 100)] <- "MPZ upper"
f.mode$Strata[which(f.mode$Depth > 700)] <- "MPZ lower"
# f.mode$Strata[which(f.mode$Depth > 1000)] <- "Bathyl"
f.mode.pooled$Strata <- factor("EZ", 
                        levels = c("EZ","MPZ upper","MPZ lower","Bathyl"))
f.mode.pooled$Strata[which(f.mode.pooled$Depth > 100)] <- "MPZ upper"
f.mode.pooled$Strata[which(f.mode.pooled$Depth > 700)] <- "MPZ lower"
# f.mode.pooled$Strata[which(f.mode.pooled$Depth > 1000)] <- "Bathyl"

print("===== SURFACE =====")

plot1 <- ggplot(
  data = f.mode.pooled,
  aes(x = Export, y = Surface, color = Location, fill = Location))+
  geom_smooth(
    method = "glm", method.args = list(family = quasibinomial()),
    se = TRUE, alpha = 0.2, color = "black", fill = "black")+
  geom_point(
    size = 3, shape = 21, color = "black")+
  facet_wrap(~ Strata, nrow = 3)+
  ylim(0,1)+
  ggtitle("Surface")
MOD1 <- glm(Surface ~ Depth + Export,
         data = f.mode,
         family = quasibinomial())
print("----- SUMMARY -----")
summary(MOD1)
print("----- ANOVA -----")
anova(MOD1)
# plot(allEffects(MOD1))

print("===== LARGE =====")
plot2 <- ggplot(
  data = f.mode.pooled,
  aes(x = Export, y = Large, color = Location, fill = Location))+
  geom_smooth(
    method = "glm", method.args = list(family = quasibinomial()),
    se = TRUE, alpha = 0.2, color = "black", fill = "black")+
  geom_point(
    size = 3, shape = 21, color = "black")+
  facet_wrap(~ Strata, nrow = 3)+
  ylim(0,1)+
  ggtitle("Large")
MOD2 <- glm(Large ~ Depth + Export,
         data = f.mode,
         family = quasibinomial())
print("----- SUMMARY -----")
summary(MOD2)
print("----- ANOVA -----")
anova(MOD2)
# plot(allEffects(MOD2))

print("===== SMALL =====")
plot3 <- ggplot(
  data = f.mode.pooled,
  aes(x = Export, y = Small, color = Location, fill = Location))+
  geom_smooth(
    method = "glm", method.args = list(family = quasibinomial()),
    se = TRUE, alpha = 0.2, color = "black", fill = "black")+
  geom_point(
    size = 3, shape = 21, color = "black")+
  facet_wrap(~ Strata, nrow = 3)+
  ylim(0,1)+
  ggtitle("Small")
MOD3 <- glm(Small ~ Depth + Export,
         data = f.mode,
         family = quasibinomial())
print("----- SUMMARY -----")
summary(MOD3)
print("----- ANOVA -----")
anova(MOD3)
# plot(allEffects(MOD3))

ggarrange(
  plot1, plot2, plot3,
  nrow = 1, common.legend = TRUE, legend = "bottom"
)
```

While anova does not identify C export flux as a significant predictor of f(surface) (p = `r anova(MOD1)[3,6]`), this is because a relationship is only observed in bathyl samples. Let's re-run this for bathyl samples alone.

```{r bathyl-Export, fig.width=8, fig.height=3, warning=FALSE}

print("===== SURFACE =====")

plot1 <- ggplot(
  data = subset(f.mode, Strata == "MPZ lower"),
  aes(x = Export, y = Surface, color = Location, fill = Location, group = Strata))+
  geom_smooth(
    method = "glm", method.args = list(family = quasibinomial()),
    se = TRUE, alpha = 0.2, color = "black", fill = "black")+
  geom_point(
    size = 3, shape = 21, color = "black")+
  facet_wrap(~ Strata, nrow = 2)+
  ylim(0,1)+
  ggtitle("Surface")
MOD1 <- glm(Surface ~ Export,
         data = subset(f.mode, Strata == "MPZ lower"),
         family = quasibinomial())
print("----- SUMMARY -----")
summary(MOD1)
print("----- ANOVA -----")
anova(MOD1)
# plot(allEffects(MOD1))

print("===== LARGE =====")
plot2 <- ggplot(
  data = subset(f.mode, Strata == "MPZ lower"),
  aes(x = Export, y = Large, color = Location, fill = Location, group = Strata))+
  geom_smooth(
    method = "glm", method.args = list(family = quasibinomial()),
    se = TRUE, alpha = 0.2, color = "black", fill = "black")+
  geom_point(
    size = 3, shape = 21, color = "black")+
  facet_wrap(~ Strata, nrow = 2)+
  ylim(0,1)+
  ggtitle("Large")
MOD2 <- glm(Large ~ Export,
         data = subset(f.mode, Strata == "MPZ lower"),
         family = quasibinomial())
print("----- SUMMARY -----")
summary(MOD2)
print("----- ANOVA -----")
anova(MOD2)
# plot(allEffects(MOD2))

print("===== SMALL =====")
plot3 <- ggplot(
  data = subset(f.mode, Strata == "MPZ lower"),
  aes(x = Export, y = Small, color = Location, fill = Location, group = Strata))+
  geom_smooth(
    method = "glm", method.args = list(family = quasibinomial()),
    se = TRUE, alpha = 0.2, color = "black", fill = "black")+
  geom_point(
    size = 3, shape = 21, color = "black")+
  facet_wrap(~ Strata, nrow = 2)+
  ylim(0,1)+
  ggtitle("Small")
MOD3 <- glm(Small ~ Export,
         data = subset(f.mode, Strata == "MPZ lower"),
         family = quasibinomial())
print("----- SUMMARY -----")
summary(MOD3)
print("----- ANOVA -----")
anova(MOD3)
# plot(allEffects(MOD3))

ggarrange(
  plot1, plot2, plot3,
  nrow = 1, common.legend = TRUE, legend = "bottom"
)
```

Just looking at bathyl samples, anova now indicates a significant relationship between f(surface) and export. Yet we do note a pattern across all three sources, where surface particles are important when flux is high, large when flux is intermediate, and small when flux is low. This is essentially an extension of the principal finding of Romero-romero et al. (2020).

## Export Ratio {-}

Before we move on to look at DVM biomass, we'll quickly look at the export ratio. This is just export flux / NPP.

```{r incorportating-ExportRatio-data}
locs <- unique(f.mode$Location)
f.mode$ExportRatio <- f.mode$Export / f.mode$NPP

locs <- unique(f.mode.pooled$Location)
f.mode.pooled$ExportRatio <- f.mode.pooled$Export / f.mode.pooled$NPP
```


```{r DepthStrata-ExportRatio, fig.width=8, fig.height=6, warning=FALSE}

f.mode$Strata <- factor("EZ", 
                        levels = c("EZ","MPZ upper","MPZ lower","Bathyl"))
f.mode$Strata[which(f.mode$Depth > 100)] <- "MPZ upper"
f.mode$Strata[which(f.mode$Depth > 700)] <- "MPZ lower"
# f.mode$Strata[which(f.mode$Depth > 1000)] <- "Bathyl"

f.mode.pooled$Strata <- factor("EZ", 
                        levels = c("EZ","MPZ upper","MPZ lower","Bathyl"))
f.mode.pooled$Strata[which(f.mode.pooled$Depth > 100)] <- "MPZ upper"
f.mode.pooled$Strata[which(f.mode.pooled$Depth > 700)] <- "MPZ lower"
# f.mode.pooled$Strata[which(f.mode.pooled$Depth > 1000)] <- "Bathyl"

print("===== SURFACE =====")

plot1 <- ggplot(
  data = f.mode.pooled,
  aes(x = ExportRatio, y = Surface, color = Location, fill = Location))+
  geom_smooth(
    method = "glm", method.args = list(family = quasibinomial()),
    se = TRUE, alpha = 0.2, color = "black", fill = "black")+
  geom_point(
    size = 3, shape = 21, color = "black")+
  facet_wrap(~ Strata, nrow = 3)+
  ylim(0,1)+
  ggtitle("Surface")
MOD1 <- glm(Surface ~ Depth + ExportRatio,
         data = f.mode,
         family = quasibinomial())
print("----- SUMMARY -----")
summary(MOD1)
print("----- ANOVA -----")
anova(MOD1)
# plot(allEffects(MOD1))

print("===== LARGE =====")
plot2 <- ggplot(
  data = f.mode.pooled,
  aes(x = ExportRatio, y = Large, color = Location, fill = Location))+
  geom_smooth(
    method = "glm", method.args = list(family = quasibinomial()),
    se = TRUE, alpha = 0.2, color = "black", fill = "black")+
  geom_point(
    size = 3, shape = 21, color = "black")+
  facet_wrap(~ Strata, nrow = 3)+
  ylim(0,1)+
  ggtitle("Large")
MOD2 <- glm(Large ~ Depth + ExportRatio,
         data = f.mode,
         family = quasibinomial())
print("----- SUMMARY -----")
summary(MOD2)
print("----- ANOVA -----")
anova(MOD2)
# plot(allEffects(MOD2))

print("===== SMALL =====")
plot3 <- ggplot(
  data = f.mode.pooled,
  aes(x = ExportRatio, y = Small, color = Location, fill = Location))+
  geom_smooth(
    method = "glm", method.args = list(family = quasibinomial()),
    se = TRUE, alpha = 0.2, color = "black", fill = "black")+
  geom_point(
    size = 3, shape = 21, color = "black")+
  facet_wrap(~ Strata, nrow = 3)+
  ylim(0,1)+
  ggtitle("Small")
MOD3 <- glm(Small ~ Depth + ExportRatio,
         data = f.mode,
         family = quasibinomial())
print("----- SUMMARY -----")
summary(MOD3)
print("----- ANOVA -----")
anova(MOD3)
# plot(allEffects(MOD3))

ggarrange(
  plot1, plot2, plot3,
  nrow = 1, common.legend = TRUE, legend = "bottom"
)
```

Export ratio turns out to be a pretty decent predictor of f(surface) (p = `r anova(MOD1)[3,6]`) and f(large) (p = `r anova(MOD2)[3,6]`), but not of f(small) (p = `r anova(MOD3)[3,6]`). 

## Diel Vertical Migrator Biomass {-}

The last predictor we will consider is DVM biomass. This is a depth specific metric, like carbon export. Again, we will use a dynamic depth cutoff defined by the base of the PPZ.

```{r incorportating-DVM-data}
locs <- unique(f.mode$Location)
f.mode$DVM <- NA
for (i in locs) {
  f.mode$DVM[which(f.mode$Location == i)] <- 
    as.numeric(data.romero$migbio.PPZ[which(data.romero$Site == i)])
}
f.mode$DVMProp <- NA
for (i in locs) {
  f.mode$DVMProp[which(f.mode$Location == i)] <- 
    as.numeric(data.romero$prop.migbio.200[which(data.romero$Site == i)])
}
locs <- unique(f.mode.pooled$Location)
f.mode.pooled$DVM <- NA
for (i in locs) {
  f.mode.pooled$DVM[which(f.mode.pooled$Location == i)] <- 
    as.numeric(data.romero$migbio.PPZ[which(data.romero$Site == i)])
}
f.mode.pooled$DVMProp <- NA
for (i in locs) {
  f.mode.pooled$DVMProp[which(f.mode.pooled$Location == i)] <- 
    as.numeric(data.romero$prop.migbio.200[which(data.romero$Site == i)])
}
```


```{r DepthStrata-DVM, fig.width=8, fig.height=6, warning=FALSE}

f.mode$Strata <- factor("EZ", 
                        levels = c("EZ","MPZ upper","MPZ lower","Bathyl"))
f.mode$Strata[which(f.mode$Depth > 100)] <- "MPZ upper"
f.mode$Strata[which(f.mode$Depth > 700)] <- "MPZ lower"
# f.mode$Strata[which(f.mode$Depth > 1000)] <- "Bathyl"

f.mode.pooled$Strata <- factor("EZ", 
                        levels = c("EZ","MPZ upper","MPZ lower","Bathyl"))
f.mode.pooled$Strata[which(f.mode.pooled$Depth > 100)] <- "MPZ upper"
f.mode.pooled$Strata[which(f.mode.pooled$Depth > 700)] <- "MPZ lower"
# f.mode.pooled$Strata[which(f.mode.pooled$Depth > 1000)] <- "Bathyl"

print("===== SURFACE =====")

plot1 <- ggplot(
  data = f.mode.pooled,
  aes(x = DVM, y = Surface, color = Location, fill = Location))+
  geom_smooth(
    data = subset(f.mode.pooled, Location != "PAP"),
    method = "glm", method.args = list(family = quasibinomial()),
    se = TRUE, alpha = 0.2, color = "black", fill = "black")+
  geom_point(
    size = 3, shape = 21, color = "black")+
  facet_wrap(~ Strata, nrow = 3)+
  ylim(0,1)+
  ggtitle("Surface")
MOD1 <- glm(Surface ~ Depth + DVM,
         data = f.mode.pooled,
         family = quasibinomial())
print("----- SUMMARY -----")
summary(MOD1)
print("----- ANOVA -----")
anova(MOD1)
# plot(allEffects(MOD1))

print("===== LARGE =====")
plot2 <- ggplot(
  data = f.mode.pooled,
  aes(x = DVM, y = Large, color = Location, fill = Location))+
  geom_smooth(
    data = subset(f.mode.pooled, Location != "PAP"),
    method = "glm", method.args = list(family = quasibinomial()),
    se = TRUE, alpha = 0.2, color = "black", fill = "black")+
  geom_point(
    size = 3, shape = 21, color = "black")+
  facet_wrap(~ Strata, nrow = 3)+
  ylim(0,1)+
  ggtitle("Large")
MOD2 <- glm(Large ~ Depth + DVM,
         data = f.mode.pooled,
         family = quasibinomial())
print("----- SUMMARY -----")
summary(MOD2)
print("----- ANOVA -----")
anova(MOD2)
# plot(allEffects(MOD2))

print("===== SMALL =====")
plot3 <- ggplot(
  data = f.mode.pooled,
  aes(x = DVM, y = Small, color = Location, fill = Location))+
  geom_smooth(
    data = subset(f.mode.pooled, Location != "PAP"),
    method = "glm", method.args = list(family = quasibinomial()),
    se = TRUE, alpha = 0.2, color = "black", fill = "black")+
  geom_point(
    size = 3, shape = 21, color = "black")+
  facet_wrap(~ Strata, nrow = 3)+
  ylim(0,1)+
  ggtitle("Small")
MOD3 <- glm(Small ~ Depth + DVM,
         data = f.mode.pooled,
         family = quasibinomial())
print("----- SUMMARY -----")
summary(MOD3)
print("----- ANOVA -----")
anova(MOD3)
# plot(allEffects(MOD3))

ggarrange(
  plot1, plot2, plot3,
  nrow = 1, common.legend = TRUE, legend = "bottom"
)
```

DVM biomass is a signifcant predictor of f(surface) (p = `r anova(MOD1)[3,6]`) and f(small) (p = `r anova(MOD3)[3,6]`), but not of f(large) (p = `r anova(MOD2)[3,6]`). 

## Summary {-}

Let's first review at some basic relationships with NPP.

```{r NPP-relationships, dpi=300, fig.width=5, fig.height=12, warning=FALSE}
ggarrange(

ggplot(
  data = subset(f.mode.pooled, Strata == "EZ"),
  aes(x = NPP, y = Export, color = Location, fill = Location, group = Strata))+
  geom_smooth(
    method = "lm", formula = y ~ poly(x, 2),
    se = TRUE, color = "black", fill="grey")+
  geom_point(
    size = 3, shape = 21, color = "black")+
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0,NA), ylim = c(0,NA))+
  theme(axis.title.x = element_blank())+
  xlim(0,80) + ylab(expression("C Export (mmol C/m"^2*"/day)"))+
  scale_fill_discrete(
    labels = c("5° N","8° N","ALOHA S","ALOHA W","Station Papa","N Atlantic")),

ggplot(
  data = subset(f.mode.pooled, Strata == "EZ"),
  aes(x = NPP, y = DVM, color = Location, fill = Location, group = Strata))+
  geom_smooth(
    method = "lm", formula = y ~ x,
    se = TRUE, color = "black", fill="grey")+
  geom_point(
    size = 3, shape = 21, color = "black")+
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0,NA), ylim = c(0,NA))+
  theme(axis.title.x = element_blank())+
  xlim(0,80) + ylab(expression("DVM Biomass (mg/m"^2*")")),

ggplot(
  data = subset(trophic.mode.mean, Depth > 200 & Depth < 1000),
  aes(x = NPP, y = baseTP, color = Location, fill = Location))+
  geom_smooth(
    data = subset(trophic.mode.mean),
    method = "lm", formula = y ~ poly(x, 2),
    se = TRUE, color = "black", fill="grey")+
  geom_point(
    size = 3, shape = 21, color = "black")+
  theme(axis.title.x = element_blank())+
  ylim(1,2.5) + xlim(0,80) + ylab("Base TP"),

ggplot(
  data = subset(trophic.mode.mean, Depth > 200 & Depth < 1000),
  aes(x = NPP, y = FWL, color = Location, fill = Location))+
  # geom_line(
  #   data = trophic.HDI95.long,
  #   aes(x = value, y = Depth, color = Size, group = interaction(Depth, Size)),
  #   alpha = 0.5, size = 1,
  #   position = position_dodgev(height = 100))+
  # geom_line(
  #   data = trophic.HDI90.long,
  #   aes(x = value, y = Depth, color = Size, group = interaction(Depth, Size)),
  #   alpha = 0.7, size = 1.5,
  #   position = position_dodgev(height = 100))+
  # geom_line(
  #   data = trophic.HDI75.long,
  #   aes(x = value, y = Depth, color = Size, group = interaction(Depth, Size)),
  #   alpha = 1, size = 2,
  #   position = position_dodgev(height = 100))+
  # geom_line(
  #   data = trophic.HDI50.long,
  #   aes(x = value, y = Depth, color = Size, group = interaction(Depth, Size)),
  #   alpha = 1, size = 3,
  #   position = position_dodgev(height = 100))+
  geom_smooth(
    data = subset(trophic.mode.mean),
    method = "lm", formula = y ~ poly(x, 2),
    se = TRUE, color = "black", fill="grey", )+
  geom_point(
    size = 3, shape = 21, color = "black")+
  theme(axis.title.x = element_blank())+
  ylim(1,2.5) + xlim(0,80) + ylab("Food Web Length"),

ggplot(
  data = subset(f.mode.pooled, Depth > 200 & Depth < 1000),
  aes(x = NPP, y = Surface, color = Location, fill = Location))+
  geom_smooth(
    method = "lm", formula = y ~ poly(x, 2),
    se = TRUE, color = "black", fill="grey", )+
  geom_point(
    size = 3, shape = 21, color = "black")+
  ylim(0,1) + xlim(0,80) + 
  ylab("f(surface)")+
  xlab(expression("NPP (mmol C/"*m^2*"/day)"))+
  scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(0,1),
    breaks = c(0,0.2,0.4,0.6,0.8,1)),

nrow = 5,
heights = c(1,1,1,1,1.2),
legend = "right",
common.legend = TRUE
)

ggarrange(

ggplot(
  data = subset(f.mode.pooled, Strata == "EZ"),
  aes(x = NPP, y = Export, color = Location, fill = Location, group = Strata))+
  # geom_smooth(
  #   method = "lm", formula = y ~ poly(x, 2),
  #   se = TRUE, color = "black", fill="grey")+
  geom_point(
    size = 3, shape = 21, color = "black")+
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0,NA), ylim = c(0,NA))+
  theme(axis.title.x = element_blank())+
  xlim(0,80) + ylab(expression("C Export (mmol C/m"^2*"/day)"))+
  scale_fill_discrete(
    labels = c("5° N","8° N","ALOHA S","ALOHA W","Station Papa","N Atlantic")),

ggplot(
  data = subset(f.mode.pooled, Strata == "EZ"),
  aes(x = NPP, y = DVM, color = Location, fill = Location, group = Strata))+
  # geom_smooth(
  #   method = "lm", formula = y ~ x,
  #   se = TRUE, color = "black", fill="grey")+
  geom_point(
    size = 3, shape = 21, color = "black")+
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0,NA), ylim = c(0,NA))+
  theme(axis.title.x = element_blank())+
  xlim(0,80) + ylab(expression("DVM Biomass (mg/m"^2*")")),

ggplot(
  data = subset(trophic.mode.mean, Depth > 200 & Depth < 1000),
  aes(x = NPP, y = baseTP, color = Location, fill = Location))+
  # geom_smooth(
  #   data = subset(trophic.mode.mean),
  #   method = "lm", formula = y ~ poly(x, 2),
  #   se = TRUE, color = "black", fill="grey")+
  geom_point(
    size = 3, shape = 21, color = "black")+
  theme(axis.title.x = element_blank())+
  ylim(1,2.5) + xlim(0,80) + ylab("Base TP"),

ggplot(
  data = subset(trophic.mode.mean, Depth > 200 & Depth < 1000),
  aes(x = NPP, y = FWL, color = Location, fill = Location))+
  # geom_line(
  #   data = trophic.HDI95.long,
  #   aes(x = value, y = Depth, color = Size, group = interaction(Depth, Size)),
  #   alpha = 0.5, size = 1,
  #   position = position_dodgev(height = 100))+
  # geom_line(
  #   data = trophic.HDI90.long,
  #   aes(x = value, y = Depth, color = Size, group = interaction(Depth, Size)),
  #   alpha = 0.7, size = 1.5,
  #   position = position_dodgev(height = 100))+
  # geom_line(
  #   data = trophic.HDI75.long,
  #   aes(x = value, y = Depth, color = Size, group = interaction(Depth, Size)),
  #   alpha = 1, size = 2,
  #   position = position_dodgev(height = 100))+
  # geom_line(
  #   data = trophic.HDI50.long,
  #   aes(x = value, y = Depth, color = Size, group = interaction(Depth, Size)),
  #   alpha = 1, size = 3,
  #   position = position_dodgev(height = 100))+
  # geom_smooth(
  #   data = subset(trophic.mode.mean),
  #   method = "lm", formula = y ~ poly(x, 2),
  #   se = TRUE, color = "black", fill="grey", )+
  geom_point(
    size = 3, shape = 21, color = "black")+
  theme(axis.title.x = element_blank())+
  ylim(1,2.5) + xlim(0,80) + ylab("Food Web Length"),

ggplot(
  data = subset(f.mode.pooled, Depth > 200 & Depth < 1000),
  aes(x = NPP, y = Surface, color = Location, fill = Location))+
  # geom_smooth(
  #   method = "lm", formula = y ~ poly(x, 2),
  #   se = TRUE, color = "black", fill="grey", )+
  geom_point(
    size = 3, shape = 21, color = "black")+
  ylim(0,1) + xlim(0,80) + 
  ylab("f(surface)")+
  xlab(expression("NPP (mmol C/"*m^2*"/day)"))+
  scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(0,1),
    breaks = c(0,0.2,0.4,0.6,0.8,1)),

nrow = 5,
heights = c(1,1,1,1,1.2),
legend = "right",
common.legend = TRUE
)

#### C Export vs NPP ####
noquote("C Export vs NPP")
LM <- lm(
  data = subset(f.mode.pooled, Strata == "EZ"),
  formula = Export ~ poly(NPP, degree = 2)
)
summary(LM)
anova(LM)

#### DVM vs NPP ####
noquote("DVM vs NPP")
LM <- lm(
  data = subset(f.mode.pooled, Strata == "EZ"),
  formula = DVM ~ NPP
)
summary(LM)
anova(LM)

#### baseTP vs NPP ####
noquote("Base TP vs NPP")
LM <- lm(
  data = subset(trophic.mode.mean, Depth > 200 & Depth < 1000),
  formula = baseTP ~ poly(NPP, degree = 2)
)
summary(LM)
anova(LM)

#### FWL vs NPP ####
noquote("FWL vs NPP")
LM <- lm(
  data = subset(trophic.mode.mean, Depth > 200 & Depth < 1000),
  formula = FWL ~ poly(NPP, degree = 2)
)
summary(LM)
anova(LM)

#### f(surface) vs NPP ####
noquote("f(surface) vs NPP")
LM <- lm(
  data = subset(f.mode.pooled, Depth > 200 & Depth < 1000),
  formula = Surface ~ poly(NPP, degree = 2)
)
summary(LM)
anova(LM)
```

```{r NPP-EXPORToverDVM, dpi=300, fig.width=5, fig.height=3, warning=FALSE}

ggplot(
  data = subset(f.mode.pooled, Strata == "EZ" & Location != "8 N"),
  aes(x = NPP, y = DVM/(Export*12), color = Location, fill = Location, group = Strata))+
  geom_smooth(
    method = "lm", formula = y ~ poly(x, 2),
    se = TRUE, color = "black", fill="grey")+
  geom_point(
    size = 3, shape = 21, color = "black")+
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0,NA), ylim = c(0,NA))+
  # theme(axis.title.x = element_blank())+
  xlim(0,80) + ylab(expression("DVM/C Export"))+
  scale_fill_discrete(
    labels = c("5° N","ALOHA S","ALOHA W","Station Papa","N Atlantic"))

ggplot(
  data = subset(f.mode.pooled, Strata == "EZ" & Location != "8 N"),
  aes(x = NPP, y = DVMProp/(Export*12), color = Location, fill = Location, group = Strata))+
  geom_smooth(
    method = "lm", formula = y ~ poly(x, 2),
    se = TRUE, color = "black", fill="grey")+
  geom_point(
    size = 3, shape = 21, color = "black")+
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0,NA), ylim = c(0,NA))+
  # theme(axis.title.x = element_blank())+
  xlim(0,80) + ylab(expression("proportion DVM/C Export (mg C)"))+
  scale_fill_discrete(
    labels = c("5° N","ALOHA S","ALOHA W","Station Papa","N Atlantic"))

ggplot(
  data = subset(f.mode.pooled, Strata == "EZ" & Location != "8 N"),
  aes(x = DVM/(Export*12), y = Surface , color = Location, fill = Location, group = Strata))+
  geom_smooth(
    method = "lm", formula = y ~ x,
    se = TRUE, color = "black", fill="grey")+
  geom_point(
    size = 3, shape = 21, color = "black")+
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0,NA), ylim = c(0,NA))+
  # theme(axis.title.x = element_blank())+
  xlab(expression("DVM/C Export"))+
  scale_fill_discrete(
    labels = c("5° N","ALOHA S","ALOHA W","Station Papa","N Atlantic"))

```

```{r NPP-relationships-allDepths, dpi=300, fig.width=5, fig.height=3, warning=FALSE}



ggplot(
  data = subset(trophic.mode.mean, Depth > 200),
  aes(x = NPP, y = FWL, color = Location, fill = Location))+
  geom_smooth(
    method = "lm", formula = y ~ poly(x, 2),
    se = TRUE, color = "black", fill="grey", )+
  geom_point(
    size = 3, shape = 21, color = "black")+
  geom_point(
    data = subset(trophic.mode.mean, Depth > 1000),
    size = 3, shape = 1, color = "purple", stroke = 2)+
  theme(axis.title.x = element_blank())+
  ylim(1,2.7) + xlim(0,80) + ylab("Food Web Length")

#### FWL vs NPP ####
noquote("FWL vs NPP")
LM <- lm(
  data = subset(trophic.mode.mean, Depth > 200),
  formula = FWL ~ poly(NPP, degree = 2)
)
summary(LM)
anova(LM)

ggplot(
  data = subset(f.mode.pooled, Depth > 200),
  aes(x = NPP, y = Surface, fill = Location))+
  geom_smooth(
    method = "lm", formula = y ~ poly(x, 2),
    se = TRUE, color = "black", fill="grey", )+
  geom_point(
    size = 3, shape = 21, color = "black")+
  geom_point(
    data = subset(f.mode.pooled, Depth > 1000),
    size = 3, shape = 1, color = "purple", stroke = 2)+
  ylim(0,1) + xlim(0,80) + 
  ylab("f(surface)")+ 
  xlab(expression("NPP (mmol C/"*m^2*"/day)"))+
  scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(0,1),
    breaks = c(0,0.2,0.4,0.6,0.8,1))

#### f(surface) vs NPP ####
noquote("FWL vs NPP")
LM <- lm(
  data = subset(f.mode.pooled, Depth > 200),
  formula = Surface ~ poly(NPP, degree = 2)
)
summary(LM)
anova(LM)

```

Let's capture the mean values at each site in a table.

```{r NPP-relationships-table}
summary <- 
  cbind(
    aggregate(
      x = cbind(NPP, DVM, Export, Surface) ~ Location,
      data = subset(f.mode.pooled, Depth > 200 & Depth < 1000), 
      FUN = "mean"
    ),
    aggregate(
      x = cbind(FWL, baseTP) ~ Location,
      data = subset(trophic.mode, Depth > 200 & Depth < 1000), 
      FUN = "mean"
    )[-1]
  )
summary <-
  # t(
    cbind(
      summary[1:3],
      round(summary[4:5],2),
      round(summary[6:7],1)
    # )
  )
kable(summary)

```


Depth is the most significant factor affecting contributions from all organic matter sources to the zooplankton food web at any given location. This is intuitive and makes sense. Surface POM becomes less important in general with depth, while sinking POM becomes increasingly important with depth. The nature of the task, then, becomes to figure out what other variable(s) predict the nature of this depth dependence.

```{r summary-depth, dpi = 300, fig.width=8, fig.height=3}
print("===== SURFACE =====")

plot1 <- ggplot(
  data = f.mode.pooled,
  aes(y = Depth, x = Surface, color = Location, fill = Location, shape = Location))+
  geom_smooth(span = 0.5,
    # method = "glm", method.args = list(family = quasibinomial()),
    # method = "lm", formula = y ~ poly(x, 2),
    se = FALSE, alpha = 0.1, orientation = "y")+
  geom_point(
    size = 3, color = "black")+
  scale_shape_manual(values = c(21,22,23,24,21,22),
                     labels = c("5° N","8° N","ALOHA S","ALOHA W","Station Papa","N Atlantic"))+
  scale_y_reverse() + 
  scale_x_continuous(position = "top",
                     breaks = c(0,0.5,1),
                     limits = c(0,1))+
  scale_color_discrete(
    labels = c("5° N","8° N","ALOHA S","ALOHA W","Station Papa","N Atlantic"))+
  scale_fill_discrete(
    labels = c("5° N","8° N","ALOHA S","ALOHA W","Station Papa","N Atlantic"))
  # ggtitle("Surface")

print("===== LARGE =====")
plot2 <- ggplot(
  data = f.mode.pooled,
  aes(y = Depth, x = Large, color = Location, fill = Location, shape = Location))+
  geom_smooth(span = 0.5,
    # method = "glm", method.args = list(family = quasibinomial()),
    # method = "lm", formula = y ~ poly(x, 2),
    se = FALSE, alpha = 0.1, orientation = "y")+
  geom_point(
    size = 3, color = "black")+
  scale_shape_manual(values = c(21,22,23,24,21,22))+
  scale_y_reverse() +
  scale_x_continuous(position = "top",
                     breaks = c(0,0.5,1),
                     limits = c(0,1)) + 
  xlab("Deep Large")
  # ggtitle("Large")

print("===== SMALL =====")
plot3 <- ggplot(
  data = f.mode.pooled,
  aes(y = Depth, x = Small, color = Location, fill = Location, shape = Location))+
  geom_smooth(span = 0.5,
    # method = "glm", method.args = list(family = quasibinomial()),
    # method = "lm", formula = y ~ poly(x, 2),
    se = FALSE, alpha = 0.1, orientation = "y")+
  geom_point(
    size = 3, color = "black")+
  scale_shape_manual(values = c(21,22,23,24,21,22))+
  scale_y_reverse() +
  scale_x_continuous(position = "top",
                     breaks = c(0,0.5,1),
                     limits = c(0,1)) + 
  xlab("Deep Small")
  # ggtitle("Small")

ggarrange(
  plot1, 
  plot2 + theme(axis.title.y = element_blank()), 
  plot3 + theme(axis.title.y = element_blank()),
  nrow = 1, common.legend = TRUE, legend = "right"
)
```

In the euphotic zone, export ratio appears to describe relevant organic matter sources well. Station Papa appears to have an anomalously high export ratio (flux:NPP) compared to other locations in the North Pacific, meaning a given amount of NPP results in greater flux into the mesopelagic zone.

```{r summary-ExportRatio, fig.width=10, fig.asp=0.3, warning=FALSE}

f.mode.pooled$Strata <- factor("EZ", 
                        levels = c("EZ","MPZ upper","MPZ lower","Bathyl"))
f.mode.pooled$Strata[which(f.mode.pooled$Depth > 100)] <- "MPZ upper"
f.mode.pooled$Strata[which(f.mode.pooled$Depth > 700)] <- "MPZ lower"
# f.mode.pooled$Strata[which(f.mode.pooled$Depth > 1000)] <- "Bathyl"
# 
print("===== SURFACE =====")

plot1 <- ggplot(
  data = subset(f.mode.pooled, Strata == "EZ"),
  aes(x = ExportRatio, y = Surface, color = Location, fill = Location, group = Strata))+
  geom_smooth(
    method = "glm", method.args = list(family = quasibinomial()),
    se = TRUE, alpha = 0.2)+
  geom_point(
    size = 3, shape = 21, color = "black")+
  facet_wrap(~ Strata, nrow = 2)+
  ggtitle("Surface")

print("===== LARGE =====")
plot2 <- ggplot(
  data = subset(f.mode.pooled, Strata == "EZ"),
  aes(x = ExportRatio, y = Large, color = Location, fill = Location, group = Strata))+
  geom_smooth(
    method = "glm", method.args = list(family = quasibinomial()),
    se = TRUE, alpha = 0.2)+
  geom_point(
    size = 3, shape = 21, color = "black")+
  facet_wrap(~ Strata, nrow = 2)+
  ggtitle("Large")

print("===== SMALL =====")
plot3 <- ggplot(
  data = subset(f.mode.pooled, Strata == "EZ"),
  aes(x = ExportRatio, y = Small, color = Location, fill = Location, group = Strata))+
  geom_smooth(
    method = "glm", method.args = list(family = quasibinomial()),
    se = TRUE, alpha = 0.2)+
  geom_point(
    size = 3, shape = 21, color = "black")+
  facet_wrap(~ Strata, nrow = 2)+
  ggtitle("Small")

plot4 <- ggplot(
  data = subset(f.mode.pooled, Strata == "EZ"),
  aes(x = NPP, y = Export, color = Location, fill = Location, group = Strata))+
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, alpha = 0.2)+
  geom_point(
    size = 3, shape = 21, color = "black")+
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0,NA), ylim = c(0,NA))+
  ggtitle("C Export vs NPP")

ggarrange(
  plot1, plot2, plot3, plot4,
  nrow = 1, common.legend = TRUE, legend = "bottom"
)
```

```{r summary-NPP, fig.width=8, fig.asp=0.4, warning=FALSE}

f.mode.pooled$Strata <- factor("EZ", 
                        levels = c("EZ","MPZ upper","MPZ lower","Bathyl"))
f.mode.pooled$Strata[which(f.mode.pooled$Depth > 100)] <- "MPZ upper"
f.mode.pooled$Strata[which(f.mode.pooled$Depth > 700)] <- "MPZ lower"
# f.mode.pooled$Strata[which(f.mode.pooled$Depth > 1000)] <- "Bathyl"

print("===== SURFACE =====")

plot1 <- ggplot(
  data = f.mode.pooled,
  aes(x = NPP, y = Surface, color = Location, fill = Location, group = Strata))+
  geom_smooth(
    method = "glm", method.args = list(family = quasibinomial()),
    se = TRUE, alpha = 0.2)+
  geom_point(
    size = 3, shape = 21, color = "black")+
  facet_wrap(~ Strata, nrow = 1)+
  ggtitle("Surface")
plot1

plot2 <- ggplot(
  data = f.mode.pooled,
  aes(x = NPP, y = Small, color = Location, fill = Location, group = Strata))+
  geom_smooth(
    method = "glm", method.args = list(family = quasibinomial()),
    se = TRUE, alpha = 0.2)+
  geom_point(
    size = 3, shape = 21, color = "black")+
  facet_wrap(~ Strata, nrow = 1)+
  ggtitle("Small")
plot2
```

Below the euphotic zone, Vertical migrator biomass is the most significant predictor of f(surface), which should absolutely not be surprising.

```{r summary-DVM, fig.width=8, fig.asp=0.4, warning=FALSE}

f.mode.pooled$Strata <- factor("EZ", 
                        levels = c("EZ","MPZ upper","MPZ lower","Bathyl"))
f.mode.pooled$Strata[which(f.mode.pooled$Depth > 100)] <- "MPZ upper"
f.mode.pooled$Strata[which(f.mode.pooled$Depth > 700)] <- "MPZ lower"
# f.mode.pooled$Strata[which(f.mode.pooled$Depth > 1000)] <- "Bathyl"

print("===== SURFACE =====")

plot1 <- ggplot(
  data = f.mode.pooled,
  aes(x = DVM, y = Surface, color = Location, fill = Location, group = Strata))+
  geom_smooth(
    method = "glm", method.args = list(family = quasibinomial()),
    se = TRUE, alpha = 0.2)+
  geom_point(
    size = 3, shape = 21, color = "black")+
  facet_wrap(~ Strata, nrow = 1)+
  ggtitle("Surface")
plot1

plot2 <- ggplot(
  data = f.mode.pooled,
  aes(x = DVM, y = Small, color = Location, fill = Location, group = Strata))+
  geom_smooth(
    method = "glm", method.args = list(family = quasibinomial()),
    se = TRUE, alpha = 0.2)+
  geom_point(
    size = 3, shape = 21, color = "black")+
  facet_wrap(~ Strata, nrow = 1)+
  ggtitle("Small")
plot2
```

Let's also check out the proportion of zooplankton doing DVM as a function of NPP.

```{r prop-DVM-vs-NPP, fig.width=5, fig.height=3, warning=FALSE}
#### f(surface) vs NPP ####
noquote("f(surface) vs NPP")
LM <- lm(
  data = subset(f.mode.pooled, Depth > 200),
  formula = Surface ~ poly(NPP, degree = 2)
)
summary(LM)
anova(LM)

ggplot(
  data = subset(f.mode.pooled, Strata == "EZ"),
  aes(x = NPP, y = DVMProp, color = Location, fill = Location, group = Strata))+
  geom_smooth(
    method = "lm", formula = y ~ x,
    se = TRUE, color = "black", fill="grey")+
  geom_point(
    size = 3, shape = 21, color = "black")+
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0,NA), ylim = c(0,NA))+
  xlab(expression("NPP (mmol C/"*m^2*"/day)"))+
  xlim(0,80) + ylab("Proportion DVM")

#### proportion DVM vs NPP ####
noquote("proportion DVM vs NPP")
LM <- lm(
  data = subset(f.mode.pooled, Strata == "EZ"),
  formula = DVMProp ~ NPP
)
summary(LM)
anova(LM)
```

Export ratio does pretty well predicting f(large) in the mesopelagic, while NPP does best with f(small) in the MPZ.

```{r summary-ExportRatio-NPP, fig.width=8, fig.asp=0.4, warning=FALSE}

f.mode.pooled$Strata <- factor("EZ", 
                        levels = c("EZ","MPZ upper","MPZ lower","Bathyl"))
f.mode.pooled$Strata[which(f.mode.pooled$Depth > 100)] <- "MPZ upper"
f.mode.pooled$Strata[which(f.mode.pooled$Depth > 700)] <- "MPZ lower"
# f.mode.pooled$Strata[which(f.mode.pooled$Depth > 1000)] <- "Bathyl"

print("===== Surface =====")
plot1 <- ggplot(
  data = f.mode.pooled,
  aes(x = ExportRatio, y = Surface, color = Location, fill = Location, group = Strata))+
  geom_smooth(
    method = "glm", method.args = list(family = quasibinomial()),
    se = TRUE, alpha = 0.2)+
  geom_point(
    size = 3, shape = 21, color = "black")+
  facet_wrap(~ Strata, nrow = 1)+
  ggtitle("Surface")
plot1

print("===== LARGE =====")
plot2 <- ggplot(
  data = f.mode.pooled,
  aes(x = ExportRatio, y = Large, color = Location, fill = Location, group = Strata))+
  geom_smooth(
    method = "glm", method.args = list(family = quasibinomial()),
    se = TRUE, alpha = 0.2)+
  geom_point(
    size = 3, shape = 21, color = "black")+
  facet_wrap(~ Strata, nrow = 1)+
  ggtitle("Large")
plot2

print("===== SMALL =====")
plot3 <- ggplot(
  data = f.mode.pooled,
  aes(x = NPP, y = Small, color = Location, fill = Location, group = Strata))+
  geom_smooth(
    method = "glm", method.args = list(family = quasibinomial()),
    se = TRUE, alpha = 0.2)+
  geom_point(
    size = 3, shape = 21, color = "black")+
  facet_wrap(~ Strata, nrow = 1)+
  ggtitle("Small")
plot3
```



# Predictors of DVM biomass? {-}

One interesting question that emerges from this analysis is, to what extent is DVM correlated with other predictors? In other words, do NPP, export or export ratio predict DVM? It's pretty easy to check, so lets fit some quick linear regressions to do this.

```{r predictors-of-DVM, fig.width=8, fig.asp=0.4, warning=FALSE}

plot1 <- ggplot(
  data = data.romero,
  aes(y = migbio.PPZ , x = `NPP (best)`, color = Site, fill = Site))+
  geom_smooth(
    method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "black", fill = "grey")+
  geom_point(
    size = 3, shape = 21, color = "black")
plot1
MOD1 <- lm(migbio.PPZ ~ `NPP (best)`,
         data = data.romero)
print("----- SUMMARY -----")
summary(MOD1)
print("----- ANOVA -----")
anova(MOD1)


plot1 <- ggplot(
  data = data.romero,
  aes(y = migbio.PPZ , x = fluxC.PPZ.avg, color = Site, fill = Site))+
  geom_smooth(
    method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "black", fill = "grey")+
  geom_point(
    size = 3, shape = 21, color = "black")
plot1
MOD1 <- lm(migbio.PPZ ~ fluxC.PPZ.avg,
         data = data.romero)
print("----- SUMMARY -----")
summary(MOD1)
print("----- ANOVA -----")
anova(MOD1)

plot1 <- ggplot(
  data = data.romero,
  aes(y = migbio.PPZ , x = fluxC.PPZ.avg/`NPP (best)`, color = Site, fill = Site))+
  geom_smooth(
    method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "black", fill = "grey")+
  geom_point(
    size = 3, shape = 21, color = "black")
plot1
MOD1 <- lm(migbio.PPZ ~ fluxC.PPZ.avg/`NPP (best)`,
         data = data.romero)
print("----- SUMMARY -----")
summary(MOD1)
print("----- ANOVA -----")
anova(MOD1)

plot1 <- ggplot(
  data = data.romero,
  aes(y = bio.200.day , x = `NPP (best)`, color = Site, fill = Site))+
  geom_smooth(
    method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "black", fill = "grey")+
  geom_point(
    size = 3, shape = 21, color = "black")
plot1
MOD1 <- lm(bio.200.day ~ `NPP (best)`,
         data = data.romero)
print("----- SUMMARY -----")
summary(MOD1)
print("----- ANOVA -----")
anova(MOD1)

plot1 <- ggplot(
  data = data.romero,
  aes(y = bio.200.day , x = fluxC.PPZ.avg, color = Site, fill = Site))+
  geom_smooth(
    method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "black", fill = "grey")+
  geom_point(
    size = 3, shape = 21, color = "black")
plot1
MOD1 <- lm(bio.200.day ~ fluxC.PPZ.avg,
         data = data.romero)
print("----- SUMMARY -----")
summary(MOD1)
print("----- ANOVA -----")
anova(MOD1)

```

Looks like NPP is a significant predictor of DVM biomass!
